_comment: Example user profile — customize these fields to match your interests. This drives what the LLM considers 'relevant' when ranking papers.
role_context: Describe yourself in 1-2 sentences so the LLM understands what lens to use (e.g., role, industry, stage of career, specific goals).
primary_interests: Topic area 1 (what you most want to hear about)
secondary_interests: Topic area that's still relevant but lower priority
wildcard_interest: Describe what would make a paper interesting even if it doesn't match your listed topics — e.g., 'genuinely novel paradigms' or 'anything with real-world benchmarks over 10x improvement'.
deprioritize: Topics you want to filter out
Narrow application areas that aren't your focus

prioritized_sources.usage_note: Papers from these sources get a ranking boost, but a brilliant paper from an unknown lab should still surface. Source is a tiebreaker, not a gatekeeper.

preference_signals.accessibility_preference: Prefer papers that introduce concepts clearly, even if technically deep
